Description.Name: The Hunt Begins
Description.Details: The Argo's command deck is crowded but focused. Marcus Hammerick and Dr. Tilsaye appear on split viewscreens. Darius speaks first. "Marcus, Dr. Tilsaye. Joanna contacted us directly. She knows we have the archive and she knows Dr. Tilsaye is no longer in her custody. During the confrontation she let something slip. She mentioned Fröislandis VII. We believe that's where the weapon is located." Marcus's expression shifts immediately. "Fröislandis VII. Even deeper into the Periphery. That has to be where she's completing final assembly." Dr. Tilsaye speaks up, her expression grave. "Before we discuss tactical plans, you need to understand what the Armageddon project actually is. The designation is misleading. Armageddon is a complete weapons platform. A mech." "An enormous superheavy mech, far larger than any BattleMech successfully constructed in human history. Previous superheavy designs were limited to approximately two hundred tons due to structural and materials constraints. This prototype exceeds that limit by a significant margin. The breakthrough came from exotic materials research. Lattice-core structural supports, rapid-form reinforcement systems. All the research Joanna forced me to complete. This is why she needed me specifically." "The prototype also incorporates Project Rotating Fury, a parallel weapons program I was never cleared to access. The security classification was higher than anything else in the Armageddon archive. Be extremely careful, Commander." Yang breaks the silence. "A mech past the two hundred ton threshold? The structural stress alone would be catastrophic. You're telling us she built something that shouldn't be able to function?" Tilsaye nods slowly. "The exotic materials solve problems that made superheavy combat units of this scale impossible before. Whether it functions as designed under combat conditions, I cannot guarantee. But Joanna believes it works. She's staking everything on that belief." Marcus takes over. "Intelligence points to Joanna activating the weapon ahead of schedule. With the archive gone and Dr. Tilsaye extracted, she's forcing her remaining researchers to accelerate final assembly. Completion is possible within days. At the same time she's pulling every [[DM.Factions[faction_RemembranceCell],Remembrance Cell]] warrior back from across the Deep Periphery. Every fanatic who believes in her vision. The facility will be heavily defended." "We need to get to Fröislandis VII before she can deploy that weapon against a major population center. Or worse, before she proves the concept and starts building more. This prototype cannot be allowed to survive." "Fröislandis VII is a small frozen planet at the edge of the system. Polar ice caps, tundra biomes. Extreme cold, low visibility, atmospheric interference on sensors. Heat dissipation will be excellent, but mobility and targeting will suffer." He pauses. "I've rallied what loyal Tribe warriors I could find who are willing to stand against her. A small force, nothing like her numbers, but it will have to be enough. Even I will be there." "Before you commit, I want you to know that we are opening our remaining supply caches to your unit. Weapons, equipment, ammunition, everything we have left. If we fail at Fröislandis VII none of it matters anyway. Consider it yours regardless of your decision." "I hope you will join us. You have already proved your combat capabilities and with your strength at our side we might stand a chance. Please accept this final contract, Commander. Let me know your decision as soon as you have one." The transmission ends. The crew looks to you for the decision.

Description.Name: There is no real choice. Accept the contract and prepare for travel to Fröislandis VII.
There is no real choice. Accept the contract and prepare for travel to Fröislandis VII.
Description.Details: Darius runs a quick readiness assessment, pulling up status reports on his console. "Boss, we're battered from the Argondale engagement. Mechs are damaged, armor needs replacement, ammunition reserves are lower than I'd like. Crew is exhausted." He looks up, something resolute in his expression. "But morale is the highest it's been since we started this campaign. Everyone knows what we're fighting for now. We stop a weapon designed to exterminate civilian populations, that gives people a purpose." Before he can continue, Sumire flags an incoming contact on the nav console. "Commander, Marcus wasn't kidding about those supply caches. We've got a Tribe cargo hauler on approach, transponder matches his authentication codes." The hauler docks within the hour. Yang supervises the offload personally. Crate after crate of Tribe equipment fills the cargo bay. Weapons, advanced electronics, ammunition in calibers they've never stocked before. All of it unmarked, all of it with the usual well above standard production quality. Yang checks the manifest and lets out a low whistle. "He wasn't holding back. There's enough exotic hardware in here to refit half the lance. Some of this I've never even seen before." Sumire pulls up navigation data. "Fröislandis VII coordinates are locked. We need to move immediately. Every day we delay gives Joanna more time to prepare her defenses or build more of those monstrosities." Farah's voice is quiet but resolute. "This is about preventing genocide. We all know what that weapon is designed to do. We stop it or millions die." Darius speaks last. "We go. One last trip before we can finish this whole sordid story and go back to our normal lives." Yang is already moving toward the mech bay. "Finally. Let's end this." [A new flashpoint has been created in the Fröislandis system to continue the campaign]
